metadata.title: Development and Licensure of Vaccines to Prevent COVID-19 — Guidance for Industry
metadata.document_version: Final Guidance (implemented without prior public comment per 21 U.S.C. 371(h)(1)(C))

rules.rule_text: COVID-19 vaccines licensed in the United States must meet statutory and regulatory requirements for quality, development, manufacture, and control including cGMP. The vaccine product must be adequately characterized and manufactured in compliance with cGMP (section 501(a)(2)(B) of the FD&C Act and 21 CFR Parts 210, 211, and 610).
rules.verbatim_quote: COVID-19 vaccines licensed in the United States must meet the statutory and regulatory requirements for vaccine development and approval, including for quality, development, manufacture, and control (section 351(a) of the Public Health Service Act (PHS Act), (42 U.S.C. 262)). The vaccine product must be adequately characterized and its manufacture in compliance with applicable standards including current good manufacturing practice (cGMP) (section 501(a)(2)(B) of the FD&C Act (21 U.S.C. 351(a)(2)(B)) and 21 CFR Parts 210, 211, and 610).

rules.rule_text: Vaccine production processes must be well defined and appropriately controlled to ensure consistency in manufacturing. Sponsors should engage in early communications with OVRR to discuss the type and extent of CMC information needed for development and licensure.
rules.verbatim_quote: It is critical that vaccine production processes for each vaccine are well defined and appropriately controlled to ensure consistency in manufacturing... FDA recommends that vaccine manufacturers engage in early communications with OVRR to discuss the type and extent of chemistry, manufacturing, and control information needed for development and licensure of their COVID-19 vaccine.
rules.exceptions: Platform technology may reduce the need for product-specific data with appropriate justification.

rules.rule_text: Source material controls must be demonstrated for all materials used in manufacturing, including history and qualification of cell banks, history and qualification of virus banks, and identification of all animal-derived materials used for cell culture and virus growth.
rules.source_section: III.B — Manufacture of Drug Substance and Drug Product
rules.verbatim_quote: Data should be provided to show that all source material used in manufacturing is adequately controlled, including, for example, history and qualification of cell banks, history and qualification of virus banks, and identification of all animal derived materials used for cell culture and virus growth.

rules.rule_text: Complete details of the manufacturing process must be provided in the BLA (21 CFR 601.2). This includes critical process parameters, critical quality attributes, batch records, defined hold times, and the in-process testing scheme. Specifications must be established for each critical parameter.
rules.source_section: III.B — Manufacture of Drug Substance and Drug Product
rules.verbatim_quote: Complete details of the manufacturing process must be provided in a Biologics License Application (BLA) to support licensure of a COVID-19 vaccine (21 CFR 601.2). Accordingly, sponsors should submit data and information identifying critical process parameters, critical quality attributes, batch records, defined hold times, and the in-process testing scheme. Specifications should be established for each critical parameter.

rules.rule_text: In-process control tests must be established that allow quality to be monitored for each lot for all stages of production (section 501(a)(2)(B) of the FD&C Act and, as applicable, 21 CFR 211.110(a)).
rules.verbatim_quote: In-process control tests must be established that allow quality to be monitored for each lot for all stages of production (section 501(a)(2)(B) of the FD&C Act (21 U.S.C. 351(a)(2)(B)) and, as applicable, 21 CFR 211.110(a)).

rules.rule_text: The manufacturing process must be adequately validated. Validation typically includes data on manufacture of at least three commercial-scale batches meeting predetermined in-process controls, critical process parameters, and lot release specifications (section 501(a)(2)(B) of the FD&C Act and 21 CFR 211.100(a) and 211.110).
rules.verbatim_quote: The manufacturing process must be adequately validated (section 501(a)(2)(B) of the FD&C Act (21 U.S.C. 351(a)(2)(B)) and, as applicable, 21 CFR 211.100(a) and 211.110). Validation would typically include a sufficient number of commercial-scale batches that can be manufactured routinely, meeting predetermined in-process controls, critical process parameters, and lot release specifications. Typically, data on the manufacture of at least three commercial-scale batches are sufficient to support the validation of the manufacturing process.

rules.rule_text: All quality-control release tests, including key tests for vaccine purity, identity, and potency, must be validated and shown to be suitable for the intended purpose. Data on the qualification/validation for all quality indicating assays must be submitted to the BLA to support licensure.
rules.verbatim_quote: All quality-control release tests, including key tests for vaccine purity, identity and potency, should be validated and shown to be suitable for the intended purpose... Data on the qualification/validation for all quality indicating assays should be submitted to the BLA to support licensure.
rules.exceptions: Release specifications are product specific and will be discussed with the sponsor as part of BLA review.

rules.rule_text: For vaccine licensure, stability and expiry date must be demonstrated using final containers from at least three final lots made from different vaccine bulks. The vaccine must maintain potency for a period equal to that from the date of release to the expiry date (21 CFR 601.2 and 610.10).
rules.verbatim_quote: For vaccine licensure, the stability and expiry date of the vaccine in its final container, when maintained at the recommended storage temperature, should be demonstrated using final containers from at least three final lots made from different vaccine bulks... The vaccine must have been shown to maintain its potency for a period equal to that from the date of release to the expiry date (21 CFR 601.2 and 610.10).
rules.exceptions: Post-marketing commitments to provide full shelf-life data may be acceptable with appropriate justification.

rules.rule_text: Storage conditions, including container closure integrity, must be fully validated (21 CFR 211.166). A product-specific stability program must be established to verify that licensed product maintains quality over the defined shelf life.
rules.verbatim_quote: Storage conditions, including container closure integrity, must be fully validated (21 CFR 211.166). A product specific stability program should be established to verify that licensed product maintains quality over the defined shelf life.

rules.rule_text: Manufacturing facilities must be of suitable size and construction to prevent contamination, cross-contamination, and mix-ups. All utilities must be validated, and HVAC systems must provide adequate control over air pressure, micro-organisms, dust, humidity, and temperature (21 CFR 211.42(a), 211.46(c)). Facility and equipment cleaning and maintenance processes must be developed and validated (21 CFR 211.56(c) and 211.67(b)).
rules.verbatim_quote: Facilities must be of suitable size and construction to facilitate operations and should be adequately designed to prevent contamination, cross-contamination and mix-ups (section 501(a)(2)(B) of the FD&C Act (21 U.S.C. 351(a)(2)(B)) and, as applicable, 21 CFR 211.42(a)). All utilities (including plumbing and sanitation) must be validated, and HVAC systems must provide adequate control over air pressure, micro-organisms, dust, humidity, and temperature, and sufficient protection or containment as needed... Facility and equipment cleaning and maintenance processes must be developed and validated.

rules.rule_text: A quality control unit must be established with responsibility for oversight of manufacturing and review and release of components, containers and closures, labeling, in-process material, and final products (21 CFR 211.22). The QC unit must have responsibility for approving validation protocols, reports, investigating deviations, and instituting corrective and preventive actions.
rules.verbatim_quote: A quality control unit must be established and must have the responsibility for oversight of manufacturing, and review and release of components, containers and closures, labeling, in-process material, and final products (section 501(a)(2)(B) of the FD&C Act (21 U.S.C. 351(a)(2)(B)) and, as applicable, 21 CFR 211.22). The quality control unit must have the responsibility for approving validation protocols, reports, investigate deviations, and institute corrective and preventive actions.

rules.rule_text: Pre-license inspections of manufacturing sites are considered part of the BLA review and are generally conducted following the acceptance of a BLA filing (21 CFR 601.20). Vaccine manufacturers should engage in early communication with CBER's Office of Compliance and Biologics Quality to discuss facility preparation and inspection timing.
rules.verbatim_quote: Pre-license inspections of manufacturing sites are considered part of the review of a BLA and are generally conducted following the acceptance of a BLA filing (21 CFR 601.20).

rules.rule_text: For COVID-19 vaccine candidates consisting of a novel product type with no prior nonclinical and clinical data available, nonclinical safety studies will be required prior to proceeding to first-in-human (FIH) clinical trials (21 CFR 312.23(a)(8)).
rules.verbatim_quote: For a COVID-19 vaccine candidate consisting of a novel product type and for which no prior nonclinical and clinical data are available, nonclinical safety studies will be required prior to proceeding to FIH clinical trials 21 CFR 312.23(a)(8).
rules.exceptions: If the vaccine candidate is made using a platform technology that has been used to manufacture a licensed vaccine and is sufficiently characterized, prior toxicology data from the same platform may be used in lieu of new nonclinical safety studies.

rules.rule_text: When needed to support FIH clinical trials, nonclinical safety assessments (including toxicity and local tolerance studies) must be conducted under GLP conditions (21 CFR Part 58). These studies must be completed and analyzed prior to initiation of FIH clinical trials.
rules.verbatim_quote: When needed to support proceeding to FIH clinical trials, nonclinical safety assessments including toxicity and local tolerance studies must be conducted under conditions consistent with regulations prescribing good laboratory practices for conducting nonclinical laboratory studies (GLP) (21 CFR Part 58). Such studies should be completed and analyzed prior to initiation of FIH clinical trials.
rules.exceptions: Data may be submitted as unaudited final draft toxicologic reports to accelerate FIH trials; final quality-assured reports must be available to FDA within 120 days of FIH trial start.

rules.rule_text: Prior to enrolling pregnant women and women of childbearing potential who are not actively avoiding pregnancy in clinical trials, sponsors must conduct developmental and reproductive toxicity (DART) studies with their respective COVID-19 vaccine candidate. Alternatively, sponsors may submit DART data from a similar product using comparable platform technology if the agency agrees.
rules.verbatim_quote: FDA recommends that prior to enrolling pregnant women and women of childbearing potential who are not actively avoiding pregnancy in clinical trials, sponsors conduct developmental and reproductive toxicity (DART) studies with their respective COVID-19 vaccine candidate. Alternatively, sponsors may submit available data from DART studies with a similar product using comparable platform technology if, after consultation with the agency, the agency agrees those data are scientifically sufficient.
rules.exceptions: DART data from a comparable platform technology may be substituted with agency agreement.

rules.rule_text: Biodistribution studies in an animal species should be considered if the vaccine construct is novel in nature and there are no existing biodistribution data from the platform technology. These studies must be conducted if there is a likelihood of altered infectivity and tissue tropism or if a novel route of administration and formulation is to be used.
rules.verbatim_quote: Biodistribution studies in an animal species should be considered if the vaccine construct is novel in nature and there are no existing biodistribution data from the platform technology. These studies should be conducted if there is a likelihood of altered infectivity and tissue tropism or if a novel route of administration and formulation is to be used.
rules.exceptions: Not required if existing biodistribution data from the same platform technology are available.

rules.rule_text: Immunogenicity studies in animal models must be conducted to evaluate the immunologic properties of the COVID-19 vaccine candidate and to support FIH clinical trials. Studies must include evaluation of humoral, cellular, and functional immune responses. Assays used for immunogenicity evaluation must be demonstrated to be suitable for their intended purpose.
rules.source_section: IV.C — Characterization of the Immune Response in Animal Models
rules.verbatim_quote: Immunogenicity studies in animal models responsive to the selected COVID-19 vaccine antigen should be conducted to evaluate the immunologic properties of the COVID-19 vaccine candidate and to support FIH clinical trials... Studies should include an evaluation of humoral, cellular, and functional immune responses, as appropriate to each of the included COVID-19 antigens... The assays used for immunogenicity evaluation should be demonstrated to be suitable for their intended purpose.

rules.rule_text: To support FIH clinical trials, sponsors must conduct studies characterizing the vaccine-induced immune response in animal models evaluating immune markers of potential vaccine-associated enhanced respiratory disease (ERD), including assessments of functional immune responses (neutralizing antibody) versus total antibody responses and Th1/Th2 balance at clinically relevant doses.
rules.source_section: IV.D — Studies to Address ERD Risk
rules.verbatim_quote: To support proceeding to FIH clinical trials, sponsors should conduct studies characterizing the vaccine-induced immune response in animal models evaluating immune markers of potential ERD outcomes. These should include assessments of functional immune responses (e.g., neutralizing antibody) versus total antibody responses and Th1/Th2 balance in animals vaccinated with clinically relevant doses of the COVID-19 vaccine candidate.

rules.rule_text: For COVID-19 vaccine candidates for which data raise increased concerns about ERD, postvaccination animal challenge data and/or animal immunopathology studies are critical to assess protection and/or ERD prior to advancing to FIH clinical trials.
rules.source_section: IV.D — Studies to Address ERD Risk
rules.verbatim_quote: For COVID-19 vaccine candidates for which data raise increased concerns about ERD, postvaccination animal challenge data and/or animal immunopathology studies are critical to assess protection and/or ERD prior to advancing to FIH clinical trials.

rules.rule_text: The primary goal of COVID-19 vaccine development programs is traditional approval via direct evidence of vaccine safety and efficacy in protecting humans from SARS-CoV-2 infection and/or clinical disease. Sponsors must submit summaries of data available at each development milestone for FDA review and concurrence prior to advancing to the next phase of development.
rules.verbatim_quote: the goal of new vaccine development programs to prevent COVID-19 should be to pursue traditional approval via direct evidence of vaccine safety and efficacy in protecting humans from SARS-CoV-2 infection and/or disease... sponsors should plan to submit summaries of data available at each development milestone for FDA review and concurrence prior to advancing to the next phase of development.
rules.exceptions: Accelerated approval based on surrogate endpoints may be considered once immune correlates of protection are established.

rules.rule_text: FIH and early-phase studies (typically 10–100 participants) must first enroll healthy adult participants who are at low risk of severe COVID-19. Some preliminary safety data in younger adults (e.g., 7 days after a single vaccination) must be available prior to enrolling older adult participants, especially for vaccine platforms without prior clinical experience.
rules.verbatim_quote: Once sufficient pre-clinical data are available, FIH and other early phase studies (which typically expose 10–100 participants to each vaccine candidate being evaluated) should first enroll healthy adult participants who are at low risk of severe COVID-19... Some preliminary safety data in younger adults (e.g., 7 days after a single vaccination) should be available prior to enrolling older adult participants, especially for vaccine platforms without prior clinical experience.
rules.exceptions: Older adult participants over 55 years may be enrolled in FIH/early phase studies if they do not have medical comorbidities associated with increased risk of severe COVID-19.

rules.rule_text: To generate sufficient data to meet the BLA approval standard, late-phase clinical trials to demonstrate vaccine efficacy with formal hypothesis testing will likely need to enroll many thousands of participants, including many with medical comorbidities for trials seeking to assess protection against severe COVID-19. Late-phase trials must be preceded by adequate characterization of safety and immunogenicity in a few hundred participants per vaccine candidate, dose level, and age group.
rules.verbatim_quote: To generate sufficient data to meet the BLA approval standard, late phase clinical trials to demonstrate vaccine efficacy with formal hypothesis testing will likely need to enroll many thousands of participants, including many with medical comorbidities for trials seeking to assess protection against severe COVID-19... Initiation of late phase trials should be preceded by adequate characterization of safety and immunogenicity (e.g., in a few hundred participants for each vaccine candidate, dose level, and age group to be evaluated).

rules.rule_text: Individuals with acute COVID-19 (or other acute infectious illness) must be excluded from COVID-19 vaccine trials. Trials need not screen for or exclude participants with history or laboratory evidence of prior SARS-CoV-2 infection.
rules.verbatim_quote: COVID-19 vaccine trials need not screen for or exclude participants with history or laboratory evidence of prior SARS-CoV-2 infection. However, individuals with acute COVID-19 (or other acute infectious illness) should be excluded from COVID-19 vaccine trials.

rules.rule_text: Later-phase trials, including efficacy trials, must be randomized, double-blinded, and placebo-controlled. A 1:1 randomization between vaccine and placebo groups is usually the most efficient study design. Follow-up of study participants for COVID-19 outcomes should continue at least one to two years to assess duration of protection and potential ERD.
rules.verbatim_quote: Later phase trials, including efficacy trials, should be randomized, double-blinded, and placebo controlled. An individually randomized controlled trial with 1:1 randomization between vaccine and placebo groups is usually the most efficient study design for demonstrating vaccine efficacy... Follow-up of study participants for COVID-19 outcomes (in particular, for severe COVID-19 disease manifestations) should continue as long as feasible, ideally at least one to two years.
rules.exceptions: If a COVID-19 vaccine proven safe and effective precludes ethical use of placebo control, that vaccine could serve as control with non-inferiority hypothesis testing.

rules.rule_text: Protocols for adaptive trials must include pre-specified criteria for adding or removing vaccine candidates or dosing regimens. Protocols for seamless trials must include pre-specified criteria (e.g., safety and immunogenicity data) for advancing from one phase of the study to the next.
rules.verbatim_quote: Protocols for adaptive trials should include pre-specified criteria for adding or removing vaccine candidates or dosing regimens, and protocols for seamless trials should include pre-specified criteria (e.g., safety and immunogenicity data) for advancing from one phase of the study to the next.

rules.rule_text: Either laboratory-confirmed COVID-19 or laboratory-confirmed SARS-CoV-2 infection is an acceptable primary endpoint. Acute cases of COVID-19 must be virologically confirmed (e.g., by RT-PCR). FDA recommends that either the primary or secondary endpoint be defined as virologically confirmed SARS-CoV-2 infection with one or more of a specified list of symptoms.
rules.verbatim_quote: Either laboratory-confirmed COVID-19 or laboratory-confirmed SARS-CoV-2 infection is an acceptable primary endpoint for a COVID-19 vaccine efficacy trial. Acute cases of COVID-19 should be virologically confirmed (e.g., by RT-PCR)... FDA recommends that either the primary endpoint or a secondary endpoint (with or without formal hypothesis testing) be defined as virologically confirmed SARS-CoV-2 infection with one or more of the following symptoms: Fever or chills; Cough; Shortness of breath or difficulty breathing; Fatigue; Muscle or body aches; Headache; New loss of taste or smell; Sore throat; Congestion or runny nose; Nausea or vomiting; Diarrhea.
rules.exceptions: Case definitions may need modification for pediatric patients and those with respiratory comorbidities; discuss with Agency prior to enrollment.

rules.rule_text: Severe COVID-19 must be evaluated as a secondary endpoint (with or without formal hypothesis testing) if not evaluated as a primary endpoint. FDA recommends severe COVID-19 be defined as virologically confirmed SARS-CoV-2 infection with: clinical signs at rest of severe systemic illness (respiratory rate ≥ 30/min, HR ≥ 125/min, SpO2 ≤ 93% on room air or PaO2/FiO2 < 300 mmHg); respiratory failure; evidence of shock; significant acute renal, hepatic, or neurologic dysfunction; ICU admission; or death.
rules.verbatim_quote: Regardless, severe COVID-19 should be evaluated as a secondary endpoint (with or without formal hypothesis testing) if not evaluated as a primary endpoint. FDA recommends that severe COVID-19 be defined as virologically confirmed SARS-CoV-2 infection with any of the following: Clinical signs at rest indicative of severe systemic illness (respiratory rate ≥ 30 per minute, heart rate ≥ 125 per minute, SpO2 ≤ 93% on room air at sea level or PaO2/FiO2 < 300 mm Hg); Respiratory failure; Evidence of shock; Significant acute renal, hepatic, or neurologic dysfunction; Admission to an ICU; Death.

rules.rule_text: The primary efficacy endpoint point estimate and lower bound confidence interval for a placebo-controlled efficacy trial must be appropriately justified. Appropriate statistical methods must be used to control type 1 error for hypothesis testing on multiple endpoints and/or interim efficacy analyses. The same statistical success criterion used for the primary analysis must be used for any interim analysis designed for early detection of efficacy.
rules.verbatim_quote: To ensure that a COVID-19 vaccine is effective, the primary efficacy endpoint point estimate for a placebo-controlled clinical disease efficacy trial and the lower bound confidence interval should be appropriately justified... For each vaccine candidate, appropriate statistical methods should be used to control type 1 error for hypothesis testing on multiple endpoints and/or interim efficacy analyses.

rules.rule_text: Late-phase studies must include interim analyses to assess risk of vaccine-associated ERD and futility. Study sample sizes and timing of interim analyses must be based on statistical success criteria for primary and secondary efficacy analyses and realistic, data-driven estimates of vaccine efficacy and COVID-19 incidence in the populations and locales where the trial will be conducted.
rules.verbatim_quote: Late phase studies should include interim analyses to assess risk of vaccine-associated ERD (see section F) and futility. Study sample sizes and timing of interim analyses should be based on the statistical success criteria for primary and secondary (if applicable) efficacy analyses and realistic, data-driven estimates of vaccine efficacy and incidence of COVID-19 (or SARS-CoV-2 infection) for the populations and locales in which the trial will be conducted.

rules.rule_text: Safety assessments throughout clinical development must include: solicited local and systemic adverse events for at least 7 days post-vaccination in an adequate number of participants; unsolicited adverse events in all participants for at least 21–28 days post-vaccination; serious and other medically attended adverse events in all participants for at least 6 months after all vaccinations; and pregnancy follow-up for conceptions within 30 days of vaccination.
rules.verbatim_quote: Safety assessments throughout clinical development should include: Solicited local and systemic adverse events for at least 7 days after each study vaccination in an adequate number of study participants to characterize reactogenicity... Unsolicited adverse events in all study participants for at least 21–28 days after each study vaccination. Serious and other medically attended adverse events in all study participants for at least 6 months after completion of all study vaccinations... All pregnancies in study participants for which the date of conception is prior to vaccination or within 30 days after vaccination should be followed for pregnancy outcomes, including pregnancy loss, stillbirth, and congenital anomalies.
rules.exceptions: Longer safety monitoring may be warranted for vaccine platforms with novel adjuvants.

rules.rule_text: The pre-licensure safety database for COVID-19 preventive vaccines must consist of at least 3,000 study participants vaccinated with the dosing regimen intended for licensure, sufficient for each of younger adult and elderly populations.
rules.verbatim_quote: The pre-licensure safety database for preventive vaccines for infectious diseases typically consists of at least 3,000 study participants vaccinated with the dosing regimen intended for licensure. FDA anticipates that adequately powered efficacy trials for COVID-19 vaccines will be of sufficient size to provide an acceptable safety database for each of younger adult and elderly populations.
rules.exceptions: This is the typical standard — additional safety concerns during development could warrant further evaluation.

rules.rule_text: COVID-19 vaccine trials must periodically monitor for unfavorable imbalances between vaccine and control groups in serious adverse events and other adverse events of special interest that may represent signals of vaccine-associated serious risks. Studies must include pre-specified criteria for halting based on the occurrence, frequency, and severity of certain adverse events.
rules.verbatim_quote: COVID-19 vaccine trials should periodically monitor for unfavorable imbalances between vaccine and control groups in serious adverse events and other adverse events of special interest that may represent signals of a vaccine-associated serious risks. Studies should include pre-specified criteria for halting based on the occurrence, frequency, and severity of certain adverse events.

rules.rule_text: FDA recommends use of an independent data safety monitoring board (DSMB) for safety signal monitoring, especially during later-stage development.
rules.verbatim_quote: FDA recommends use of an independent data safety monitoring board (DSMB) (Ref. 16) for safety signal monitoring, especially during later stage development.

rules.rule_text: Routine post-licensure pharmacovigilance for licensed COVID-19 vaccines includes expedited reporting of serious and unexpected adverse events and periodic safety reports in accordance with 21 CFR 600.80 (Postmarketing reporting of adverse experiences).
rules.source_section: VI.B — Pharmacovigilance Activities for COVID-19 Vaccines
rules.verbatim_quote: Routine pharmacovigilance for licensed biological products includes expedited reporting of serious and unexpected adverse events as well as periodic safety reports in accordance with 21 CFR 600.80 (Postmarketing reporting of adverse experiences).

rules.rule_text: At the time of a BLA submission for a COVID-19 vaccine, applicants must submit a Pharmacovigilance Plan (PVP) as described in the FDA Guidance for Industry on E2E Pharmacovigilance Planning. The PVP must include actions to address all important identified risks, important potential risks, and important missing information.
rules.verbatim_quote: FDA recommends that at the time of a BLA submission for a COVID-19 vaccine, applicants submit a Pharmacovigilance Plan (PVP) as described in the FDA Guidance for Industry; E2E Pharmacovigilance Planning... The PVP should include actions designed to address all important identified risks, important potential risks or important missing information.

rules.rule_text: FDA may require postmarketing studies or trials for COVID-19 vaccines to assess known or potential serious risks under section 505(o)(3) of the FD&C Act (21 U.S.C. 355(o)(3)) when such studies or trials are warranted. Such studies may be required at the time of approval or after approval if FDA becomes aware of new safety information.
rules.verbatim_quote: For COVID-19 vaccines, FDA may require postmarketing studies or trials to assess known or potential serious risks when such studies or trials are warranted... Section 505(o)(3) of the FD&C Act (21 U.S.C. 355(o)(3)) authorizes FDA to require certain postmarketing studies or clinical trials for... biological products approved under section 351 of the PHS Act (42 U.S.C. 262).

rules.rule_text: Diagnostic assays used to support the pivotal efficacy analysis (e.g., RT-PCR) must be sensitive and accurate for the purpose of confirming infection and must be validated before use.
rules.verbatim_quote: Diagnostic assays used to support the pivotal efficacy analysis (e.g., RT-PCR) should be sensitive and accurate for the purpose of confirming infection and should be validated before use.

rules.rule_text: Assays used for immunogenicity evaluation must be suitable for their intended purpose of assessing relevant immune responses to vaccination and must be validated before use in pivotal clinical trials.
rules.verbatim_quote: Assays used for immunogenicity evaluation should be suitable for their intended purpose of assessing relevant immune responses to vaccination and be validated before use in pivotal clinical trials.

rules.rule_text: Accelerated approval of a COVID-19 vaccine based on a surrogate endpoint may be considered under section 506 of the FD&C Act and 21 CFR 601.40 if an applicant provides sufficient data on adequate and well-controlled clinical trials establishing an effect on a surrogate endpoint reasonably likely to predict clinical benefit. For drugs granted accelerated approval, postmarketing confirmatory trials are required and must usually be underway at the time of accelerated approval.
rules.source_section: VIII.A — Additional Considerations in Demonstrating Vaccine Effectiveness
rules.verbatim_quote: accelerated approval of a COVID-19 vaccine pursuant to section 506 of the FD&C Act (21 U.S.C. 356) and 21 CFR 601.40 may be considered if an applicant provides sufficient data and information to meet the applicable legal requirements... For drugs granted accelerated approval, postmarketing confirmatory trials have been required to verify and describe the predicted effect on clinical benefit. These studies should usually be underway at the time of the accelerated approval (see section 506(c)(2) of the FD&C Act (21 U.S.C. 356(c)(2)) and 21 CFR Part 601, Subpart E).
rules.exceptions: Accelerated approval is only available once additional understanding of SARS-CoV-2 immunology and vaccine immune responses that might predict protection is acquired.

rules.rule_text: A potential surrogate endpoint for COVID-19 vaccine accelerated approval would depend on the characteristics of the vaccine (antigen structure, mode of delivery, antigen processing). Since SARS-CoV-2 is a novel pathogen, a surrogate endpoint should ideally be derived from human efficacy studies examining clinical disease endpoints. If derived from other data sources, sponsors must consult FDA to reach agreement.
rules.source_section: VIII.A — Additional Considerations in Demonstrating Vaccine Effectiveness
rules.verbatim_quote: A potential surrogate endpoint likely would depend on the characteristics of the vaccine, such as antigen structure, mode of delivery, and antigen processing and presentation in the individual vaccinated... Since SARS-CoV-2 represents a novel pathogen, a surrogate endpoint reasonably likely to predict protection from COVID-19 should ideally be derived from human efficacy studies examining clinical disease endpoints. If the surrogate endpoint is derived from other data sources, sponsors should consult the FDA to reach agreement on the use of the surrogate endpoint.
